securityFindings.finding: Path traversal via unsanitized feature slug — pre-existing codebase pattern, CLI-only local usage
SHIP_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS prefix matching without path normalization — mitigated by git diff clean paths
Double-quoted git commit -m placeholder allows shell expansion — recommend heredoc for commit message

performanceFindings.finding: git diff subprocess spawned twice in build_ship_draft — should compute once and thread through
Plan/summary/review JSONs re-read 2-3x each — should pre-compute shared data

evidence: D1: SHIP_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS hardcoded with all required runtime paths
D2: generate_commit_message reads plan commitMessage field, no LLM re-summarization
D3: generate_pr_body produces terse sections (Summary/Test Plan/Review/Planning References), Follow-ups only on warn
D4: Auto-infer guarded by branch match + git rev-parse HEAD + run uniqueness, fails closed
D5: Module at ship_draft.py, CLI run-ship-draft in workflow_memory.py
D6: /ship command updated to use draft-driven flow
D7: .gitignore already excludes work-orders/*.json, runs/, feature-phases.json
D8: build_ship_draft returns commitSurface[] and gitAddCommand

findings.detail: code-reviewer noted generate_pr_body reads 'command' key but SUMMARY.json uses 'commands' (plural) — test fixtures may mask this

stageReviews.notes: All 8 decisions correctly implemented. 42/42 tests pass. One minor schema key mismatch noted.

evidence: code-reviewer: Module structure follows orchestration patterns, stdlib-only respected
security-scanner: subprocess calls use list-form args (no shell=True), 3 WARN findings (slug validation, prefix normalization, commit message quoting)
perf-analyzer: 2 WARN findings (duplicate subprocess spawn, redundant JSON reads) — recommend pre-computing shared data
